mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, providing a comprehensive platform for managing the entire AI lifecycle. It is modular and scalable, allowing users to deploy specific components or the entire platform based on their needs. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and management versus the flexibility and power it offers for AI workloads.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow command-line interface (CLI) to manage your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xvf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1 --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Update kubeconfig with cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Kubeflow.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resources allocated to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeflow has not been installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure Kubeflow is installed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI lifecycle components and improved integration with existing tools.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs and components.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to update your configurations to match the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
